subject: Fix OpenDaylight Yangtools RFC7950Reactors call
commit_message:
Fix OpenDaylight Yangtools RFC7950Reactors call

defaultReactor() is no more directly usable since Aluminium SR1
and must be replaced by defaultReactorBuilder()

https://jira.opendaylight.org/browse/YANGTOOLS-1085

Issue-ID: SDNC-1515
Signed-off-by: Guillaume Lambert <guillaume.lambert@orange.com>
Change-Id: Idb876c96c855ec72469bccb014850f1df7eef438

Patch Set 3:

Hi Ravi

Dan is blocked by an issue similar to the one you already got with YangToolsParser
as described in https://jira.opendaylight.org/browse/YANGTOOLS-1202
Any help and advice is welcome.

Thanks in advance  !

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3:
> > 
> > Hi Ravi
> > 
> > Dan is blocked by an issue similar to the one you already got with YangToolsParser
> > as described in https://jira.opendaylight.org/browse/YANGTOOLS-1202
> > Any help and advice is welcome.
> 
> Thanks in advance  !

Hi Guillaume, 

   The issue that I reported in ODL jira is different than the one currently encountered. In my case, I was using a specific Yang parser model as a dependency in the pom which I am not supposed to, as that model is not exposed to the external world but used internally by the code. It was marked as package-private in the MANIFEST and that resulted in the exception. In this particular case, it is not clear to me what changed that led to this problem and from when we have this problem. I am trying with some previous versions to see if they are any different. Will let you know if there is any progress.

Regards,
Ravi

In my case, I was using a specific Yang parser model as a dependency in the pom which I am not supposed to, as that model is not exposed to the external world but used internally by the code. It was marked as package-private in the MANIFEST and that resulted in the exception. In this particular case, it is not clear to me what changed that led to this problem and from when we have this problem. I am trying with some previous versions to see if they are any different. Will let you know if there is any progress.
> 
> Regards,

Thanks Ravi for the feedback.
So different causes led to the same error.

Meanwhile, we got more details from Yangtools maintainer by email at
https://lists.opendaylight.org/g/kernel-dev/message/412
It seems that the code has to evolve to use an instance of YangParserFactory through OSGI services.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-2

This fix still uses an internal Yangtools API which is no longer supported in Aluminum.  Instead, we have to access the YangParserFactory via OSGi.  Working on a fix suggested by Robert Varga, which I'll push separately.

Patch Set 3:

This is fixed in this review : https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ccsdk/sli/+/120653
